# UIshades.com

> Free, ad-free generator for tints and shades of any color. Convert any hex,
> rgb(), hsl(), oklch(), or CSS color name into a 20-step OKLCH perceptual ramp
> or an 11-stop Tailwind scale (50–950), with copy-ready exports. Mirrors
> 0to255.com's URL structure: /[hex] and /colors/[name] permanent links.

This is the expanded reference. For the short version, see https://uishades.com/llms.txt

## What UIshades is

UIshades.com turns a single color into a usable palette. Paste a hex, an
`rgb()`, `hsl()`, or `oklch()` value, or a CSS color name (like `coral`), and
it generates a perceptual ramp of tints and shades plus an 11-stop Tailwind
scale you can drop straight into a design system. No ads, no signup, nothing to
pay for. A WCAG contrast badge is shown on every shade.

## The two palettes

### Tailwind scale (default)
An 11-stop scale at the familiar Tailwind stops: 50, 100, 200, 300, 400, 500,
600, 700, 800, 900, 950. The input color snaps to its nearest stop (the
"anchor stop"), and the remaining stops are derived around it. This is the
default view and the server-rendered content on `/` and `/[hex]`.

### OKLCH ramp
A 20-shade perceptual ramp at evenly spaced lightness, from about L=0.95 down
to L=0.06. There are no pure `#ffffff` / `#000000` endpoints — the L=0.06 lower
bound keeps achromatic inputs off pure black after sRGB rounding. The input hex
is pinned verbatim to the inner step whose target lightness is closest to the
input's measured lightness. Chroma follows a bell-curve multiplier (1.0 at mid
lightness, about 0.3 at the extremes) so the ramp stays inside the sRGB gamut
and avoids washed-out clipping near white and black.

## URL structure

- `/` — home; the tool itself, seeded with a default color.
- `/[hex]` — permanent page for any 3-, 6-, or 8-digit hex (e.g. `/4040ff`).
- `/colors/[name]` — one page per CSS named color (~209 of them), each with a
  ramp, aliases, a harmony palette, and WCAG contrast badges (e.g.
  `/colors/coral`).
- `/p/[slug]` — a public saved palette by slug (optional human feature).

## Exports

Every palette can be copied or downloaded as:

- Tailwind v4 `@theme` block
- Tailwind v3 config
- CSS custom properties (CSS variables)
- W3C Design Tokens (DTCG) JSON
- Figma Variables
- PNG image of the palette ("Download PNG")

## JSON API

Read-only, public, no authentication.

`GET https://uishades.com/api/{hex}.json` — `{hex}` is a bare 3/6/8-digit hex
(no `#`), e.g. `4040ff`. Returns the full color data: the OKLCH ramp, the
Tailwind scale, and lighter/darker neighbors. Invalid hex returns 404.

Tiny example:

```
GET https://uishades.com/api/4040ff.json

{
  "input": "#4040ff",
  "ramp": { "mode": "oklch", "shades": [ /* 20 shades */ ], "inputIndex": 11 },
  "scale": { "shades": [ /* 11 shades, stops 50–950 */ ], "anchorStop": 500 },
  "neighbors": { "lighter": ["#5050ff"], "darker": ["#3030ef"] }
}
```

Each shade is `{ "hex": "#rrggbb", "oklch": { "l": 0.5, "c": 0.2, "h": 264 } }`;
scale shades also carry a `stop` (50–950), and the pinned input shade carries
`"isInput": true`.

There is also `GET https://uishades.com/api/p/{slug}.json` for a public saved
palette by slug.

OpenAPI description: https://uishades.com/openapi.json
API catalog: https://uishades.com/.well-known/api-catalog

## MCP server

`POST https://uishades.com/mcp` — Model Context Protocol over streamable HTTP,
JSON-RPC 2.0, no authentication.

Tool: `generate_shades`
- Input: `{ "color": string }` — a hex, `rgb()`/`hsl()`/`oklch()`, or CSS color
  name.
- Output: the same color data as the JSON API, returned as markdown text plus
  `structuredContent` (the ColorPageData object).

Server card: https://uishades.com/.well-known/mcp/server-card.json

## Notes

- Everything is free, ad-free, and requires no signup. See
  https://uishades.com/.well-known/auth.md for the (lack of) auth details.
- Hex values are canonical lowercase `#rrggbb` throughout the data model.
